papers.title: DriveVLM: Vision-Language Model for End-to-End Autonomous Driving with Multi-Modal Reasoning
papers.summary: We present DriveVLM, a vision-language model that performs end-to-end autonomous driving with explicit multi-modal reasoning. DriveVLM integrates visual perception, language understanding, and action prediction in a unified framework, enabling interpretable driving decisions.

papers.title: WorldDrive: Predictive World Models for Robust Autonomous Driving in Unstructured Environments
papers.summary: WorldDrive introduces a novel predictive world modeling approach for autonomous driving that learns to anticipate future scene evolution and agent behaviors. The model demonstrates improved robustness in unstructured environments compared to traditional methods.

papers.title: BEV-Transformer++: Enhanced Bird's Eye View Perception with Temporal Fusion for Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: BEV-Transformer++ extends previous BEV perception methods with enhanced temporal fusion mechanisms, achieving state-of-the-art performance on nuScenes and Waymo Open Dataset for 3D object detection and tracking.

papers.title: DiffusionDrive: Diffusion Policies for Smooth and Safe Autonomous Driving Maneuvers
papers.summary: DiffusionDrive applies diffusion models to autonomous driving policy learning, generating smooth and safe trajectories through iterative denoising. The approach shows advantages in handling multi-modal driving scenarios and uncertainty.

papers.title: SparseDriveV2: Scoring is All You Need for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: End-to-end multi-modal planning has been widely adopted to model the uncertainty of driving behavior, typically by scoring candidate trajectories and selecting the optimal one.

papers.title: ExploreVLA: Dense World Modeling and Exploration for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: End-to-end autonomous driving models based on Vision-Language-Action (VLA) architectures have shown promising results by learning driving policies through behavior cloning on expert demonstrations.

papers.title: Vision-Language-Action Models for Autonomous Driving: Past, Present and Future
papers.summary: Survey of VLA models for autonomous driving, summarizing representative datasets and benchmarks for evaluating VLA-based driving systems.

papers.title: Uni-World VLA: Interleaved World Modeling and Planning for Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: Unified vision-language-action (VLA) model that tightly integrates world modeling and planning for autonomous driving.
